title: Access to information - Canada.ca
content:
## Access to Information and Privacy - ESDC
The Access to Information and Privacy (ATIP) Division is responsible for the administration of the Access to Information Act and the Privacy Act throughout the Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC) portfolio, including Service Canada and the Labour Program.

### Personal Information in My Service Canada Account
You can view most of your personal information conveniently with your My Service Canada Account (MSCA) . MSCA is where you will find information such as:
- Canada Pension Plan (CPP) Statement of Contributions
- Canada Pension Plan (CPP), Old Age Security (OAS) or Employment Insurance (EI)  Payments
- Social Insurance Number information - you can view your SIN directly and get guidance on what to do if you've lost your card
- Record of Employment (ROE)
- Some tax slips: T4E for Employment Insurance (EI), T4A/NR4 for Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS)

### Tax Information in CRA My Account
You can also conveniently view your personal income tax and benefit information and manage your tax affairs with your CRA My Account .
If the information you are seeking is not available in your My Service Canada Account or your CRA My Account, you may submit an Access to Information or Privacy request. Here’s how:
Requests for records held by ESDC should be submitted under the Access to Information Act and must be accompanied by a $5.00 application fee. Requests to access personal information about individuals, including your own, held by the department should be submitted under the Privacy Act. Please note that there are no fees for submitting Privacy Act requests.
Whether your request is submitted pursuant to the Privacy Act or the Access to Information Act, your request should clearly indicate the type of record(s) you are seeking and include timeframes where possible. Please note that these requests have a legislated 30 day service standard and may require an extension depending on the request submitted. Be specific and include all relevant information. This will assist the Department in locating the appropriate records.
- Learn  more about submitting a request for information

## Completed Access to Information requests
ESDC posts a list of completed access to information requests online. All requests related to ESDC, Service Canada, Labour, and the Canada Employment Insurance Commission are processed by ESDC and are included in these statistics.
- Browse our  completed ATI summaries

## Privacy notice statements
- ESDC Consultation and engagement activities privacy notice statement
- Security video surveillance system privacy notice statement

## Reports, publications and policies
ESDC provides annual reports on the administration of the Access to Information Act and Privacy Act, develops privacy policies, and posts privacy impact assessments.
- Browse all  of our ATI Reports and Publications
